title: Build Agent Capacity Rebalancing
description: A build-and-CI governance workflow that enforces stage gates, capacity policies, and quality/security controls before pipeline advancement. Focus case: build agent capacity rebalancing.
assumptions: Agents and pools are represented as discrete resources with accurate availability state.
Approvals and automated checks are atomic and can be modeled as boolean predicates.
Capacity profiles, node groups and security controls are pre-defined and compatible with pool membership mappings.
risks: Stale availability state may cause double-allocation of agents if external state diverges.
Insufficiently granular approvals could allow promotion without required checks, violating policy.
Incorrect mapping of quotas to workflows can lead to resource starvation or overcommit.
